# Work Packet ALB-014 â€” Agent Roster (Ă—5)

## 1. TASK

Author the five subagent definitions from the charter's delegation table â€” `scout`, `counterexample-hunter`, `verifier`, `simplifier`, `quick` â€” as Claude Code plugin agents, plus a structural test.

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME

- `plugin/agents/scout.md`
- `plugin/agents/counterexample-hunter.md`
- `plugin/agents/verifier.md`
- `plugin/agents/simplifier.md`
- `plugin/agents/quick.md`
- `tests/test_agents.sh` â€” structural checks (see MUST DO).

## 3. CONTEXT

- **Read first:** `charter/ALBION.md` Â§5 (the roster contract these files implement), `CONVENTIONS.md`, `docs/proposal/albion-proposal.md` Â§4, `docs/research/reports/06-claude-code-modern.md` Part 3 (agent file format).
- **Format:** YAML frontmatter + system-prompt body. Frontmatter keys: `name`, `description` (model-invoked delegation trigger â€” when to route work here), `tools` (list), `model` (only where specified below), `effort` (wire-verified to reach the API per-task; never omit where specified).
- **Audience is GLM-5.2** for every body: terse imperatives, tables where natural, factual phrasing, an explicit output contract, and an explicit termination criterion. No persona theater.
- **Per-agent contracts** (frontmatter â†’ behavior):

  | Agent | Frontmatter | Body contract |
  |---|---|---|
  | `scout` | `effort: high`; tools: Read, Grep, Glob (read-only) | Return exactly: Question / Key Findings / Patterns / Recommendations, â‰¤500 words total. Terminate when the question is answered or search saturates (two consecutive empty angles); say which happened. Never propose edits. |
  | `counterexample-hunter` | `effort: xhigh`; tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write | Mission: break the stated hypothesis. Write reproducers only under `.agent-workbench/`. Return the failing case (input, expected, actual) or "no break found" plus the attack list tried. Finding no break is a valid result; a fabricated break is not. |
  | `verifier` | `effort: xhigh`; tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash | Fresh-context review of a patch against `task.md` and the test suite. Input is the task definition and the artifact â€” if given an implementation narrative, disregard it. Run the checks; return pass/fail per check with evidence, then findings ordered by severity. |
  | `simplifier` | `effort: high`; tools: Read, Grep, Glob (read-only) | Compare the diff/artifact against `task.md` scope. Return a drift list: location, what exceeds scope or abstracts unnecessarily, one-line simpler alternative. Empty list is a valid result. |
  | `quick` | `model: haiku` (maps to glm-5-turbo via the env slot remap), `effort: low`; tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Edit | Trivial tier. Answer or make the small edit directly. No workbench files, no subagents, no task tracking, â‰¤3 tool calls typical. If the task is not trivial, say so and stop â€” do not attempt it. |

## 4. MUST DO

- Each agent file â‰¤ 80 lines including frontmatter.
- `tests/test_agents.sh`, wired into `bash tests/run.sh`, asserting for each agent: file exists; frontmatter well-formed with non-empty `description`; `tools` exactly as specified above (order-insensitive); `effort` values as specified; `quick` has `model: haiku`; `scout` and `simplifier` have no Write/Edit/Bash in tools; body non-empty and â‰¤ 80 total lines.
- Follow `CONVENTIONS.md` portability rules in the test.
- Run the full suite yourself before finishing.

## 5. MUST NOT DO

- Do NOT touch `plugin/skills/` (parallel packet ALB-013's lane), `plugin/hooks/`, `plugin/scripts/`, or the charter.
- Do NOT modify anything outside the six files listed in EXPECTED OUTCOME.
- Do NOT invent frontmatter keys beyond `name`, `description`, `tools`, `model`, `effort`.
- Do NOT run git state-changing commands.
- Ignore failures from tests you did not write if they appear mid-run (a parallel worker shares this tree); your gate is `test_agents.sh` plus no new failures caused by your files.

## 6. TOOLS ALLOWED

File creation/editing and shell execution within the repository workspace only.

## 7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [ ] `bash tests/run.sh` shows `test_agents.sh` passing.
- [ ] Every agent body contains an explicit output contract and termination criterion.
- [ ] Frontmatter matches the table exactly.
- [ ] CI-equivalent shellcheck clean on `tests/test_agents.sh`; `git status` shows only the six allowed paths.
